evals.name: new-js-rest-api-load-test
evals.prompt: I need to write a k6 load test for our login API in JavaScript. The endpoint is POST /api/v1/auth/login with a JSON body of username and password. After login I need to call GET /api/v1/profile using the returned token. We expect 100 concurrent users at peak and our SLA is p95 under 800ms. I have a CSV file with test users.
evals.expected_output: A complete runnable JavaScript k6 script following the 5-block pattern (options with scenarios+thresholds, setup for auth, SharedArray for CSV users, default function with groups and sleep, teardown). Uses ramping-vus executor. Thresholds include p(95)<800. Run command with BASE_URL env var included.

assertions.name: uses_5_block_pattern
assertions.description: Script has all 5 blocks: options (scenarios+thresholds), setup, SharedArray, default function with groups/checks/sleep, teardown or at minimum a complete runnable structure

assertions.name: uses_ramping_vus_executor
assertions.description: Executor is ramping-vus (not constant-vus) to ramp up to 100 VUs, not instant spike

assertions.name: has_threshold_p95_800
assertions.description: Thresholds include p(95)<800 matching the stated SLA

assertions.name: token_extracted_from_setup
assertions.description: Auth token is obtained in setup() and passed to default function via data parameter

assertions.name: has_sleep_between_requests
assertions.description: default function includes at least one sleep() call between requests

assertions.name: uses_shared_array
assertions.description: CSV user data is loaded via SharedArray, not via open() inside default function

assertions.name: includes_run_command
assertions.description: Response includes the k6 run command with --env BASE_URL parameter

assertions.name: checks_do_not_replace_thresholds
assertions.description: Script uses both check() for per-request diagnostics AND thresholds for test pass/fail — not check() alone

evals.name: fix-checks-not-failing-test
evals.expected_output: Explains that check() records statistics but never fails the test. To fail the test, thresholds must be defined in options. Delivers corrected script with both check() kept for diagnostics and thresholds added (http_req_failed rate<0.01 and checks rate>0.99). Explains the three mechanisms: check() vs threshold vs fail().

assertions.name: identifies_root_cause
assertions.description: Explains that check() never stops or fails the test — it only records pass/fail statistics

assertions.name: adds_thresholds
assertions.description: Fixed script adds export const options with thresholds block including http_req_failed and/or checks rate threshold

assertions.name: keeps_check_for_diagnostics
assertions.description: check() is kept in the fixed script alongside thresholds — not removed

assertions.name: explains_three_mechanisms
assertions.description: Explains the difference between check(), threshold, and fail() and when to use each

evals.name: wrong-executor-for-rps-target
evals.prompt: I need my k6 test to maintain exactly 200 requests per second for 10 minutes to validate our API's throughput capacity. I'm using ramping-vus but the RPS keeps varying depending on response time. What's the right way to do this?
evals.expected_output: Explains the open vs closed workload model difference. Delivers a script using constant-arrival-rate executor with rate:200, timeUnit:'1s', correct preAllocatedVUs calculation (rate × p95_seconds × 1.2), and maxVUs as safety ceiling. Explains dropped_iterations and how to detect them.

assertions.name: explains_open_vs_closed
assertions.description: Explains why ramping-vus (closed model) varies throughput with response time, and why arrival-rate (open model) maintains fixed RPS

assertions.name: uses_constant_arrival_rate
assertions.description: Fixed script uses constant-arrival-rate executor, not ramping-vus

assertions.name: sets_rate_200
assertions.description: rate is set to 200 matching the stated requirement

assertions.name: includes_preAllocatedVUs
assertions.description: preAllocatedVUs is set with a reasonable value and explanation of how to calculate it

assertions.name: includes_maxVUs
assertions.description: maxVUs is set as a safety ceiling above preAllocatedVUs

assertions.name: mentions_dropped_iterations
assertions.description: Warns about dropped_iterations metric and how to detect/prevent it

evals.name: memory-issue-large-data-file
evals.expected_output: Identifies root cause: open() and JSON.parse() inside default function copies the 50MB file into memory for every VU on every iteration. Fix is to use SharedArray in init context — loaded once, shared across all VUs. Delivers corrected script with SharedArray. Explains deterministic vs random selection with idInTest.

assertions.name: identifies_root_cause
assertions.description: Explains that open() and JSON.parse() inside default() copies data per VU per iteration, multiplying memory usage by VU count × iterations

assertions.name: uses_shared_array
assertions.description: Fixed script uses SharedArray initialized at init context (top level), not inside default function

assertions.name: import_in_init_context
assertions.description: import of SharedArray and open() call are at the top level of the file, not inside a function

assertions.name: explains_memory_benefit
assertions.description: Explains that SharedArray loads data once and shares the reference — not copied per VU

evals.name: websocket-load-test
evals.prompt: I need to load test a WebSocket chat API. The flow is: connect to wss://chat.example.com/ws with a JWT token in the header, send a message every 5 seconds, validate that the server echoes back within 200ms, and disconnect after 30 seconds. We need to simulate 50 concurrent connections.
evals.expected_output: Complete k6 script using k6/ws. Uses constant-vus with 50 VUs. Connection includes Authorization header. setInterval for sending messages every 5s. check on message roundtrip time. setTimeout for graceful disconnect after 30s. Error handler registered. Checks validated.

assertions.name: imports_k6_ws
assertions.description: Script imports from k6/ws correctly

assertions.name: uses_constant_vus_50
assertions.description: Options use constant-vus executor with 50 VUs

assertions.name: auth_header_in_connection
assertions.description: ws.connect() params include Authorization header with JWT token

assertions.name: uses_set_interval_for_heartbeat
assertions.description: socket.setInterval() is used to send messages every 5 seconds

assertions.name: validates_roundtrip_in_message_handler
assertions.description: socket.on('message') handler includes a check for response latency < 200ms

assertions.name: has_error_handler
assertions.description: socket.on('error') handler is registered

assertions.name: uses_set_timeout_to_close
assertions.description: socket.setTimeout() is used to close the connection after ~30 seconds
